mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-09-01 PAYEMS at 139,435k indicates sustained post-crisis employment recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with PAYEMS growth > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above its 12-month trend, real rates compress into negative territory, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and bonds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-09-01 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 237.428 indicate near-zero nominal rates with moderate inflation
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of 60-day forward returns, or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% signals economic deceleration, which reduces corporate profit growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-04-01 GDPC1 at 16,010.4B indicates ongoing expansion but subject to quarterly volatility
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with growth deceleration risk
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 2%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction of more than 1% month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings downside.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-08-01 INDPRO at 104.1152 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of months with INDPRO contracting > 1%, or the mechanism produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.1 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,000k units combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth > 3% year-over-year signals robust consumer demand and housing investment, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through higher corporate revenues and profit margins.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-08-01 HOUST at 956k and RSAFS at 442,689M indicate strong housing recovery and retail demand
regime_hint: post-crisis demand recovery with synchronized housing and consumption growth
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances where both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-09-01 UNRATE at 5.9% reflects ongoing labor market normalization
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of 3-month periods with UNRATE rising > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains unchanged signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression, triggering equity outperformance over bonds and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged for 2+ months
evidence_refs: fact:2014-09-01 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% with CPIAUCSL at 237.428 indicates policy accommodation
failure_condition: Interaction term p-value > 0.10 in logistic regression, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 57% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2% combined with housing starts (HOUST) falling below 900k units signals synchronized demand weakness across consumption and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-04-01 GDPC1 at 16,010.4B and 2014-08-01 HOUST at 956k indicate expansion but subject to synchronized weakness risk
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration with synchronized demand contraction
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where both conditions are met simultaneously.
